item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can see a handful of clunky sentences (mostly having to do with the word "seem") but the concept is intriguing. I have an inkling on what it all means but it's not a satisfying answer to me.

+1 for an interesting premise and a hefty attempt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
View Crit
Collapse Crit

Having read this over a couple of times, I feel like I'm missing something. It's evident that whoever made this had some reason to give it its anomalous properties. I think this is meant to be a mystery, but I see no clues to the answer. It's a plot thread without conclusion. Equally, I have no idea where the D-Class go, nor can I understand why on earth the Foundation would waste so many lives for this when they already know what's going to happen. This is costly research they gain little to nothing from. I have reason to want to know what is going on here, but this doesn't tell a coherent enough story for me to make a guess.

This also suffers from having tons of fluff. Is there any value whatsoever to knowing the creator possessed " a box of chinese finger traps and a smooth sphere, which after testing appears to be made of active uranium with small flecks of red citrine quartz"? I don't think so. The test log serves little purpose beyond repeatedly reminding me it is impossible to use or interact with; this log is far longer than it needs to be. Plus, a lot of it is made redundant by the description.

The list of tapes seems to be trying to give some explanation or justification, but it really doesn't for me. I don't see how any of it is related to the creator beyond being a series of useless hints about this person mixed in with nonsense that goes nowhere. How does this: "This tape contains a 30 minute video of an adult man sitting on a bench in a park attempting to solve a Rubix cube." contribute anything to the story? All it does is distract me from what is going on, and I cannot find a reason to justify the existence of it and similarly meaningless tapes.

My takeaway from this is thus: a man in a vore cult with family problems and a dead dog has an epiphany spurred on by a sermon and prevents people from looking at his tapes. This makes very little sense to me, so I am left baffled, rather than afraid.

Additionally, there's this:

Addendum-4076-3: List of tapes found with SCP-4076:

Isn't SCP-4076 one of the tapes? Do you mean the room?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a soft spot for video scips. +1 Can we get some lip reading experts in to transcribe that sermon?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my absolute favorite new articles, and I can't even say for sure why. I think it's because this is exactly the kind of storytelling I hope to get from a lot of these articles: just enough evidence to indicate that something is going on, but not enough to even begin to figure out what.

item.title: Am I missing something?
item.content:encoded: Why exactly is the page image all static-y? Is it an effect of the entity?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are totally right, will fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I will love to go through at some point and fix errors, thanks for the offer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great Scip, especially for a first article. It's simple but in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You use customary instead of metric when referring to the silver cross. You'll probably get people complaining about that, so you might want to change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So happy to see this finally posted and being well-received to boot! It's an interesting one, and I think in the end you managed to get the balance between intriguingly mysterious and frustratingly opaque right.

You absolutely can and should go through for grammar fixes. If I see you in IRC we can go through it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I look forward to more from you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I thought I was good with the spelling and wording and stuff but of course as soon as I posted it I became aware of a million tiny errors I had missed, so I need to fix that up. I am really glad that you enjoyed the SCP though. I really tried to make it good, and I wanted it to make people feel how some of the first SCPs I read made me feel, so I am glad it made you feel that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I think you are right, I will probably fix that in a bit once I gather a few other errors to fix as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the ████████ police department

I think the words "police department" should be capitalized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is obviously something more here than meets the eye, but the second addenda felt more like it was just a random order of creepy things than a coherent story.

But that might be what I like about this, since I got a genuine spook out of this. SCP-4076-10 really made me think for a second.

Not bad for a first SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really needs some more proofreading for mild errors and clunky sentences, but holy shit I don't remember the last time an SCP gave me that chill up my spine. May have just been reading alone on a still cold night, but I honestly got a little scared to finish reading the final addendum. Agreed that this has a fantastic old school Series I or II horror vibe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor nitpick but shouldn't Back to the Future be capitalized?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I am glad you liked it. I initially didn't plan all of the addendums, but it just seemed boring and bare without it, and I felt I couldn't convey anything interesting or unique with just the basic description, so I added the addendums and tweaked them many times. Also, you are right in that my phrasing probably wasn't the best, hopefully I can do better with my future skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I just went through and fixed a bunch of grammar errors. Also, I do know that VHS tapes have not been around for hundreds of years, that was intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a very interesting read. The additional tapes add a lot of character and atmosphere to it. However, a lot of things could be phrased more professionally, especially the footnote. Overall though, really good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed it, but there are a few errors, namely in the tape logs (such as "here" instead of "there", or a missing period after "Unable to be played").

It's bizarre, since this seems like a rather mundane concept, but it has intrigue. +1

EDIT: Also, a bit strange that a VHS tape could be hundreds of years old despite their public use in the 1970s. Even magnetic tapes started being used only in the 1950s. Not sure if this is intentional, which may be a problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much. I am really glad that that is what you got from this since that is definitely what I was going for. I had a hard time getting that balance right, so I am glad to hear that it was right for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. I think you are probably right but I am not sure if I can/should edit that now that I have already posted it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I am glad that it really struck you. I hope you enjoy my future skips too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I call things like this Mr. Jones SCPs, because something is happening here but I don't know what it is. There's almost but not quite enough information to make a guess, which is a delicate balance. Definitely a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'm glad you enjoyed it. I was really going for the kind of article where people would have to spend time really thinking hard about if they wanted an answer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Left me thinking: Woah. What the actual fuck?

Great skip, IMO. A few grammar and spelling errors, but that is made up for by the idea. I will wait for more of your skips, yoissy.

Good luck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting SCP. Shouldn't "foundation" be capitalized though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It'll be interesting to see how this one fares. It's very reminiscent of early creepypasta-type scips, but in a way that I get a very real enjoyment from; it's the sort of article that I'd have spent ages poring over when I first joined the site. Well done, yoissy. Not half bad for a first attempt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good work, I really enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi. This is the first SCP I have ever written. I am really happy to finally share with all you guys after having read and enjoyed so much of what you all have made. I had a hard time writing this article, and I workshopped it over and over again before I felt it was ready to be posted, so I am really grateful to all the different people who helped me write this, especially Meserach, who helped me many different times and whose advice was essential in the creation of this article. I hope you guys enjoy my article.

Image Credit goes to Jeff Carter on Flikr. Here is the link to the original image:
https://www.flickr.com/photos/cartercomics/53317755/in/photolist-5Aeaq-9PxNo-5HgvD-5zoRp-5HgvF-5YTYr-g3NR9-5Aeap-5KDEE-8QvcC-5w3H6-9PwKQ-k2yXv-awfqv-8u2oB-5NMKY-58tyf-nPSGj-5WtVq-bPqAu-5Dets-jACmL-67VDp-cu5Gf-4ARpF-5HgvE-8ZKK2-jACmJ-6Muif-baPEJ-5WtVo-6Muie-ghZQ9-ipeN3-nPTkM-4MJyf-aVtHE-8tZYz-ghZQa-5WtVp-5KDEF-kycz1-6AyuH-7eQhK-9PxNp-exTYS-5H9ig-aVtHG-omv7N-5w
